proposal: Fix the recurring ESPN F1 mis-dating quirk at the source: f1.js now re-stamps each near-term Grand Prix with its actual RACE session date/time (from ESPN's core-API session list) instead of the Friday FP1 date ESPN puts on the scoreboard, and clears the premature FINAL status. Fail-open + bounded to the ~35-day window the pipeline keeps. Adds tests/f1-fetcher.test.js (5 cases).
evidence: coverage-audit.json (2026-07-06): TWO high-severity F1 gaps — Belgian GP board-dated fre. 17. juli 11:30 and Hungarian GP fre. 24. juli 11:30 (FP1) instead of the Sunday races 19./26. juli. sourceIssues flags 'ESPN F1 scoreboard mis-daterer GP-helger til fredagens trening'.
events.json: British Grand Prix (race Sun 5 Jul) was absent from the static board and had to be back-filled by ai-research; Belgian/Hungarian carried the wrong Friday time+TBD venue.
source-quirks skill: ESPN F1 'race weekends (high)' entry — the Friday-dated weekend drops off the board by Saturday and Sunday's race silently disappears; observed 2026-07-05 (British GP).

Mechanism confirmed live: ESPN scoreboard event 600057439 dates to 2026-07-17T11:30Z (FP1, STATUS_FINAL) while the core-API competitions list has the Race session at 2026-07-19T13:00Z (type id 3 / abbreviation 'Race').
npm test: 207 passed incl. 5 new F1 cases (re-stamp, clear-status, two fail-open paths, out-of-window no-call, recent-past drop-off case).
